what_it_does: Acceptance test suite for the run_reduce cross-unit JOIN engine. It exercises stable read-back of run:// map outputs, deterministic rule-based joining, seeded-vector clustering, live-or-mocked reduce-role synthesis, fail-loud missing-address handling, declared skip semantics, and driver-only rollup telemetry.
observations: Tests run_reduce in rule, cluster, and role variants plus the shared _read_back helper.
Uses seeded embedding vectors so clustering logic can be verified without a live embedder service.
Probes a resident 4B endpoint and falls back to mocking the fabric client to exercise the real compose+validate path.
Asserts stable sorted unit_id order for read-back, not seed or finish order.
Requires exactly one cognition.reduce rollup and one op.run run-index emit per reduce invocation.
Discovers the reduce_synth role through the file-based RoleRegistry and checks its spec shape.
Includes a deliberate vector dimension-mismatch case to verify fail-loud cosine reuse.
Uses temporary FsStore instances and run:// address seeding for isolated test data.
standouts: Seeded-vector cluster test that proves deterministic cosine grouping without a live embedder.
Live-or-mocked role variant that captures the real C 1/4 input-axis compose and schema-validate round-trip.
Strict single-rollup emit discipline (one cognition.reduce + one op.run per reduce).
Fail-loud read-back behavior shared with the run_jury pattern.
conventions: Uses a module-level check(label, cond) helper that asserts and counts passing checks.
Discovers roles via RoleRegistry and validates reduce_synth spec fields explicitly.
Sorts unit IDs for deterministic ordering and uses alphabetical tiebreaks in rule joins.
Creates isolated FsStore instances under tempfile directories for each test section.
Restores patched cognition.client.complete in a finally block after the mocked role test.
Defines small local helpers (_rendered, seeded_embed, _cos, bad_dim_embed) close to their consumers.
concerns: The live probe _try_live catches all Exception broadly, which could mask unexpected errors.
The test patches cognition.client.complete globally and relies on finally for restoration.
Cluster vector lookup depends on exact json.dumps(value, sort_keys=True) rendering of unit outputs.
The PASS counter is a mutable global incremented by check, which could be fragile if tests run in parallel.
Several test helpers are defined inline inside sections rather than as reusable fixtures.
notes: An acceptance test that proves run_reduce joins map outputs deterministically across rule, cluster, and role modes while staying fail-loud and emitting only driver-level telemetry.
questions: Where are run_reduce, _read_back, _cluster_units, and ReduceResult implemented and what are their exact contracts?
What is the full reduce_synth role specification and how is its output schema enforced?
How is the live embedder service launched and wired into production cluster mode?
Who consumes the op.run run-index emit and what does an empty addresses list imply downstream?
Does on_missing='skip' apply uniformly across rule, cluster, and role modes?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it proves read-back, deterministic rule joins, seeded clustering, live-or-mocked role synthesis, fail-loud/skip behavior, and driver-only emits.
apparent_themes: Reuse of shared primitives across jury and reduce paths
intention_signals: The file is trying to establish run_reduce as a first-class, tested cross-unit join primitive that wires the previously declared reduce-tree THOUGHT_SHAPE into a deterministic, observable, and safe implementation.
novelty: It seeds embedding vectors to test clustering logic without requiring a live embedder, and it mocks the fabric client at the complete boundary so the real compose and schema-validate path is exercised rather than bypassed.
contribution: A concrete acceptance specification for run_reduce behavior across rule, cluster, and role modes, including deterministic read-back, fail-loud and skip semantics, seeded-vector cluster verification, live-or-mocked role synthesis, and driver-only emit shape.
summary_for_embedding: Acceptance test for the run_reduce cross-unit JOIN engine covering read-back ordering, deterministic rule joins, seeded-vector clustering, live-or-mocked reduce-role synthesis, fail-loud missing addresses, and single rollup telemetry.
intention_for_embedding: To prove that run_reduce correctly joins map outputs via rule, cluster, and role variants while remaining deterministic, observable, and fail-loud.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global pass counter and prints an ok message.
symbols.description: check(label, cond) asserts the supplied condition, raising with the label on failure, and increments the module-level PASS counter to report total passing checks at the end.

symbols.does: Seeds map outputs into a temporary FsStore as run:// addresses.
symbols.description: _seed(store, turn_id, units) writes each unit value via put_content and set_ref, producing a mapping from unit_id to its run:// address so downstream reduce tests can read back through the canonical resolver.

symbols.does: Pure deterministic reduce rule implementing majority vote with alphabetical tiebreak.
symbols.description: majority_label(values) counts labels across read-back units, selects the highest count, and breaks ties alphabetically so the result is independent of input order or arrival timing.

symbols.does: Returns a stable JSON string used as the seeded vector lookup key.
symbols.description: _rendered(value) calls json.dumps with sort_keys=True, producing the exact text that the live embedder would receive for a dict-valued unit so seeded_embed can map it to a precomputed vector.

symbols.does: Injects precomputed vectors for rendered unit texts and fails loud on unknown inputs.
symbols.description: seeded_embed(texts) looks up each text in _SEED_VECS and returns the corresponding vector list, raising KeyError if any text was not pre-seeded so the test never silently uses a zero vector.

symbols.does: Local cosine similarity sanity check for seeded vectors.
symbols.description: _cos(a, b) computes the cosine between two vectors to verify that the seeded near-duplicate vectors are close to 1.0 and the distinct pair is close to 0.0 before running the cluster variant.

symbols.does: Returns intentionally mismatched-dimension vectors to test dimension fail-loud behavior.
symbols.description: bad_dim_embed(texts) produces a list where the second vector has a different dimension than the others, exercising the reused cosine path's expectation of uniform dimensionality.

symbols.does: Probes the resident 4B endpoint to decide whether to run the role variant live or mocked.
symbols.description: _try_live() opens an OpenAI-compatible transport and issues a minimal completion call; if it raises, the test falls back to a mocked fabric client so the role compose path is still exercised.

symbols.does: Small wrapper class mimicking a pydantic-style model_dump return for the mock client.
symbols.description: class _Validated holds a result dictionary and exposes model_dump() so the mocked _client.complete return value behaves like the schema-validated object the real path would produce.
__init__(self, d) assigns the supplied dictionary to self._d so it can later be returned unchanged by model_dump.

symbols.does: Returns a shallow copy of the wrapped dictionary.
symbols.description: model_dump(self) returns dict(self._d), mimicking the interface expected by callers of client.complete without depending on pydantic.

symbols.does: Mock fabric client complete that captures composed messages and returns a schema-valid reduce summary.
symbols.description: _mock_complete(t, msgs, ...) records the final user message and system prompt into a captured dict, then returns a _Validated object containing a summary string, allowing the test to verify the real compose and role-template wiring.
